narrative: Dovish Fed bets drive a broad dollar retreat as softer US jobs data fuels expectations for policy easing, lifting gold higher +1.47% and supporting global equities +0.00%. The greenback's slide anchors the week's dominant G10 theme as NZD outperforms USD by a 1.538% spread; NZD/USD rises to 0.5717 despite a -150bp OIS carry disadvantage. Meanwhile, USD/JPY consolidates at 161.02, where a +268bp OIS carry advantage keeps the pair elevated despite rising options hedging demand shown by a -1.09 25d RR. DXY retreats -0.11% while VIX consolidates at 15.9. Watch the RBNZ rate decision on July 8 for the next major G10 policy catalyst.
